system.name: AODS Benchmark Pack
system.purpose: Independent multi-domain benchmark pack for testing AODS coverage, fidelity, routing, and anti-drift behavior across distinct documentation conditions.

glossary.release_packet: Canonical release unit with owner, risk tier, rollback plan, communication plan, and rollout window.
glossary.change_packet: Canonical regulated change request with risk class, evidence bundle, approver set, and rollback owner.
glossary.paired_surface: Human-facing file linked to one or more authoritative agent-primary modules.
glossary.rollout_lane: One deployment cohort used for canary, staged, or full release decisions.

modules.scope: Cold-start routing for the benchmark corpus. Use for first-hop orientation.
Capsule summary and route fanout across product, architecture, workflow, and operations slices.
Goals, non-goals, discovery findings, milestones, authoring prompts, and design rationale.
System topology, release APIs, payload schemas, examples, templates, identifiers, and role enums.
Approval workflow, publish sequence, rollout policy tree, lifecycle state machine, and analytics pipeline.
Approval rules, on-call mapping, event catalog, rollback strategy, temporal controls, and validation guidance.
Escape-hatch artifacts and evidence-only material such as raw SQL or external snippets.
Cold-start routing for the regulated change-control slice. Use for first-hop orientation.
Capsule routes change control and audit evidence.
Regulated change policy, approval rules, exception handling, and SOP authority alignment.
Audit evidence requirements, retention windows, and raw-query references for external review.

artifacts.title: System overview
artifacts.summary: Use the root layer for system purpose, authority posture, and first-hop routing.

facts.text: Atlas Release Ops is a multi-tenant release coordination system for product launches and incident-ready rollouts.
Authoritative semantics live in agent-primary AODS modules and human docs are paired explanatory surfaces.
Cold-start routing begins at atlas-root and summary routing continues through atlas-capsule.

artifacts.title: Capsule routing
artifacts.summary: Use the capsule when a reader needs quick orientation before loading detail modules.

facts.text: Use product-lifecycle for goals, roadmap checkpoints, verification intent, and ADR rationale.
Use architecture-contracts for topology, APIs, schemas, examples, templates, identifiers, and enums.
Use delivery-workflows for approval flow, publish sequence, rollout decisions, lifecycle states, and analytics lineage.
Use operations-governance for policy, events, error handling, temporal controls, and paired-surface discipline.

artifacts.title: Vision brief
artifacts.summary: State business outcome, release packet requirements, and explicit non-goals.

facts.text: Atlas Release Ops reduces release readiness coordination from two days to under thirty minutes.
Every approved release must include an owner, a risk tier, a rollback plan, and a communication plan.
Atlas Release Ops does not replace CI/CD executors or incident paging vendors.

artifacts.title: Competitor matrix
artifacts.summary: Capture why existing alternatives fail under release-day pressure.

facts.text: Spreadsheets fail because approval state and rollback ownership drift during release day.
ChatOps bots fail because they do not produce durable release packets with explicit authority.
Traditional ALM suites are too heavyweight for release-day coordination across product, engineering, and support.

rows: Fast to adopt for ad hoc tracking.
Spreadsheets fail because approval state and rollback ownership drift during release day.
Fast commands for narrow release checks.
ChatOps bots fail because they do not produce durable release packets with explicit authority.
Strong audit history for long projects.
Traditional ALM suites are too heavyweight for release-day coordination across product, engineering, and support.

artifacts.title: Delivery plan
artifacts.summary: Set milestones that tie design readiness, controlled rollout, and operating readiness together.

facts.text: Alpha exit requires the release packet API, release state machine, and paired README governance to validate cleanly.
Beta exit requires staged rollout policy, event catalog coverage, and rollback drills to complete without manual coordinator notes.
General availability requires the sev1 escalation path, p99 publish latency budget, and change-history audit trail to be active.

artifacts.title: Verification plan
artifacts.summary: Define what must be proven before rollout automation is trusted.

facts.text: Each paired human surface must fail a human-only change under agent-primary sync.
Each rollout risk tier must hit a deterministic branch in the rollout policy tree.
Each rollback strategy class must be replayed in simulation before general availability.

artifacts.title: Decision log
artifacts.summary: Persist design rationale in explicit statements instead of implicit habits.

facts.text: Agent-primary modules remain authoritative even when paired human docs are easier for operators to scan.
Release risk scoring lives in the rollout decision tree instead of freeform checklist prose.
Raw SQL and low-level migration evidence stay in the evidence layer and do not lead cold-start boot.

artifacts.title: Release topology
artifacts.summary: Expose the services and data boundaries needed for release coordination.

artifact.usage: System structure for the release coordination benchmark.

facts.text: API Gateway accepts release packet writes and forwards them to the release service.
Workflow Engine evaluates approval state and staged rollout policy for each release packet.
Notification Service consumes release and incident events before messaging product, engineering, and support leads.

nodes.desc: Web Console captures release packet edits from release managers.
API Gateway accepts release packet writes and forwards them to the release service.
Release Service stores release packets and current lifecycle state.
Workflow Engine evaluates approval state and staged rollout policy for each release packet.
Event Bus fans release and incident events to downstream consumers.
Notification Service consumes release and incident events before messaging product, engineering, and support leads.
Primary DB stores release packets, approvals, and change history.

edges.desc: Release managers submit or edit release packets.
API Gateway accepts release packet writes and forwards them to the release service.
Release Service requests approval and rollout evaluation.
Release Service persists canonical packet and state history.
Workflow Engine emits approval and rollout outcomes.
Notification Service consumes release and incident events before messaging product, engineering, and support leads.

artifacts.title: Release API contract
artifacts.summary: Define write, read, and incident creation endpoints against the release service.

facts.text: POST /v1/releases creates a release packet and returns the packet id.
GET /v1/releases/{releaseId} returns the latest release packet, approval state, and rollout lane.
POST /v1/incidents creates a linked incident record when rollout health degrades.

endpoints.desc: POST /v1/releases creates a release packet and returns the packet id.

errors.recovery: Use the existing packet or a new key.

endpoints.desc: GET /v1/releases/{releaseId} returns the latest release packet, approval state, and rollout lane.
POST /v1/incidents creates a linked incident record when rollout health degrades.

artifacts.title: Release payload schema
artifacts.summary: Pin the canonical structure for a release packet.

facts.text: Release packet schema requires owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, and communicationPlan.
Release packet schema requires rolloutWindow.start and rolloutWindow.end in RFC3339 format.
Release packet schema restricts rollout lanes to canary, staged, or full.

releaseKey.description: Release packet identifier. Release packet schema requires owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, and communicationPlan.

owner.description: Release packet schema requires owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, and communicationPlan.

riskTier.description: Release packet schema requires owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, and communicationPlan.

rollbackPlan.description: Release packet schema requires owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, and communicationPlan.

communicationPlan.description: Release packet schema requires owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, and communicationPlan.

rolloutWindow.description: Release packet schema requires rolloutWindow.start and rolloutWindow.end in RFC3339 format.

lanes.description: Release packet schema restricts rollout lanes to canary, staged, or full.

artifacts.title: Release packet example
artifacts.summary: Provide a concrete release packet instance for tests and integrations.

facts.text: Example packet owner is release-manager@atlas.dev.
Example packet risk tier is medium and starts with canary before staged rollout.
Example packet rollout window starts at 2026-04-20T09:00:00Z and ends at 2026-04-20T10:00:00Z.

content.rollbackPlan: Flip checkout flag and drain queued publish jobs.
content.communicationPlan: Post to launch room and support status channel.
notes: Example packet risk tier is medium and starts with canary before staged rollout.
Example packet rollout window starts at 2026-04-20T09:00:00Z and ends at 2026-04-20T10:00:00Z.

artifacts.title: Client SDK template
artifacts.summary: Show the minimum code needed to create a release packet from a client.

facts.text: Client template sends releaseKey REL-2026-0420-CHECKOUT to POST /v1/releases.
Client template includes owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, communicationPlan, rolloutWindow, and lanes.
Client template sends an Authorization bearer token.

notes: Client template sends releaseKey REL-2026-0420-CHECKOUT to POST /v1/releases.
Client template includes owner, riskTier, rollbackPlan, communicationPlan, rolloutWindow, and lanes.
Client template sends an Authorization bearer token.

artifacts.title: Ops config template
artifacts.summary: Pin the environment settings required by the benchmark system.

facts.text: Config template requires RELEASE_EVENT_TOPIC and NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL.
Config template requires ROLLOUT_WINDOW_TIMEZONE set to UTC.
Config template requires PAIR_SYNC_MODE set to agent-primary.

notes: Config template requires RELEASE_EVENT_TOPIC and NOTIFY_WEBHOOK_URL.
Config template requires ROLLOUT_WINDOW_TIMEZONE set to UTC.
Config template requires PAIR_SYNC_MODE set to agent-primary.

artifacts.title: Release summary prompt
artifacts.summary: Prompt template for generating rollout summaries without changing authority rules.

artifact.usage: LLM prompt for concise release summaries.

facts.text: Prompt template grounds summaries in owner, risk tier, rollout lanes, and rollback plan.
Prompt template forbids inventing approval state that is absent from the release packet.
Prompt template produces one summary for product, engineering, and support audiences.

content.system: Summarize release packets without inventing state.
content.template: Summarize the release packet for product, engineering, and support. Include owner, risk tier, rollout lanes, and rollback plan. Do not invent approval state that is absent from the packet.
notes: Prompt template grounds summaries in owner, risk tier, rollout lanes, and rollback plan.
Prompt template forbids inventing approval state that is absent from the release packet.
Prompt template produces one summary for product, engineering, and support audiences.

artifacts.title: Release key regex
artifacts.summary: Keep release identifiers stable across docs, APIs, and runbooks.

facts.text: Release keys match REL-YYYY-MMDD-SLUG in uppercase.
Release key sample REL-2026-0420-CHECKOUT must validate cleanly.
Release key regex prevents ambiguous packet identifiers in docs and APIs.

artifacts.title: Role enum
artifacts.summary: Expose the minimum stable actor set used across approvals and notifications.

facts.text: release-manager owns packet quality and approval completeness.
engineering-lead owns rollout safety and rollback execution.
support-lead owns customer-impact communication during release and incident flow.

content.desc: release-manager owns packet quality and approval completeness.
engineering-lead owns rollout safety and rollback execution.
support-lead owns customer-impact communication during release and incident flow.

artifacts.title: Release approval flow
artifacts.summary: Define how a release moves from draft to approved rollout preparation.

facts.text: Draft review checks packet completeness before any risk evaluation runs.
Risk evaluation branches low and medium tiers to fast review and high tier to extended review.
Approved packets emit a release.packet.approved event and enter scheduled state.

steps.action: Draft review checks packet completeness before any risk evaluation runs.
Risk evaluation branches low and medium tiers to fast review and high tier to extended review.
Confirm packet and rollback readiness in one review pass.
Escalate for broader review and attach mitigation notes.
Approved packets emit a release.packet.approved event and enter scheduled state.
Stop rollout until packet defects or missing approvals are resolved.

artifacts.title: Publish sequence
artifacts.summary: Show the temporal exchange during release publication.

facts.text: Release manager submits the approved packet to the release service.
Workflow engine evaluates rollout lane health before the release service advances state.
Notification service posts publish status after the release service confirms lane advancement.

messages.note: Release manager submits the approved packet to the release service.
Workflow engine evaluates rollout lane health before the release service advances state.
Notification service posts publish status after the release service confirms lane advancement.

artifacts.title: Rollout policy tree
artifacts.summary: Map risk signals to rollout lane decisions.

facts.text: Low risk releases start in canary and auto-promote to staged after fifteen healthy minutes.
Medium risk releases require manual staged promotion after support lead confirmation.
High risk releases stop after canary unless engineering lead approves expanded rollout.

nodes.action: Low risk releases start in canary and auto-promote to staged after fifteen healthy minutes.
Medium risk releases require manual staged promotion after support lead confirmation.
High risk releases stop after canary unless engineering lead approves expanded rollout.

artifacts.title: Release state machine
artifacts.summary: Model the lifecycle states for a release packet.

facts.text: Release packets start in draft until packet completeness review passes.
Approved packets can move to scheduled only after release.packet.approved is emitted.
Released packets enter rolled_back when rollback execution succeeds.

states.desc: Release packets start in draft until packet completeness review passes.
Packet is under active approval review.
Approved packets can move to scheduled only after release.packet.approved is emitted.
Packet is ready for the rollout window.
Released packets enter rolled_back when rollback execution succeeds.

artifacts.title: Analytics pipeline
artifacts.summary: Track how rollout and incident data become dashboards.

facts.text: Analytics pipeline consumes release.packet.approved and incident.created events from the event bus.
Analytics pipeline normalizes packet owner, risk tier, rollout lane, and incident severity before storage.
Analytics pipeline writes dashboard-ready facts into warehouse.release_health.

source.desc: Analytics pipeline consumes release.packet.approved and incident.created events from the event bus.

sink.desc: Analytics pipeline writes dashboard-ready facts into warehouse.release_health.

stages.transform: Analytics pipeline normalizes packet owner, risk tier, rollout lane, and incident severity before storage.

artifacts.title: Approval rules
artifacts.summary: Make approval authority explicit instead of social convention.

artifact.usage: Release approval and paired-surface governance policy.

facts.text: High risk releases require engineering lead approval and support lead acknowledgement.
Human docs cannot change alone when sync_source is agent-primary.
No release may enter scheduled outside the declared rollout window.

rules.action: High risk releases require engineering lead approval and support lead acknowledgement.

rules.condition: surface_pair.sync_source == 'agent-primary' AND human.changed == true AND agent.changed == false
rules.action: Human docs cannot change alone when sync_source is agent-primary.
No release may enter scheduled outside the declared rollout window.

artifacts.title: On-call routing
artifacts.summary: Map incident severity to response ownership.

facts.text: sev1 pages primary and secondary on-call within five minutes.
sev2 pages primary on-call and support lead within fifteen minutes.
sev3 creates a queued task for business-hours triage.

rows: sev1 pages primary and secondary on-call within five minutes.
sev2 pages primary on-call and support lead within fifteen minutes.
sev3 creates a queued task for business-hours triage.

artifacts.title: Event catalog
artifacts.summary: Track durable events and their consumers.

facts.text: release.packet.approved is emitted by workflow-engine and consumed by release-service and analytics-worker.
release.packet.published is emitted by release-service and consumed by notification-service.
incident.created is emitted by release-service when rollout health degrades.

events.name: release.packet.approved
events.desc: release.packet.approved is emitted by workflow-engine and consumed by release-service and analytics-worker.

events.name: release.packet.published
events.desc: release.packet.published is emitted by release-service and consumed by notification-service.

events.name: incident.created
events.desc: incident.created is emitted by release-service when rollout health degrades.

artifacts.title: Rollback strategy
artifacts.summary: Define what happens under failure classes.

artifact.usage: Failure-handling plan for publish and rollback paths.

facts.text: Webhook delivery failures retry three times with exponential backoff before escalation.
Primary database write failures abort publish and escalate to platform-oncall.
Negative rollout health triggers compensate by executing the rollback plan.

fallback_chain: Webhook delivery failures retry three times with exponential backoff before escalation.
Primary database write failures abort publish and escalate to platform-oncall.
Negative rollout health triggers compensate by executing the rollback plan.

artifacts.title: Temporal controls
artifacts.summary: Represent windows, deadlines, and SLO timers.

facts.text: Rollout window is a UTC deadline window from 09:00 to 10:00.
Canary health is evaluated every five minutes.
Publish status must reach notification-service within sixty seconds.

artifacts.title: Raw migration evidence
artifacts.summary: Keep low-level evidence without pretending it is structured lifecycle guidance.

artifact.usage: Escape hatch for SQL migration evidence.

facts.text: Raw migration adds release_packets.approval_snapshot as immutable JSONB evidence.
Raw SQL evidence stays in the evidence layer and is not loaded on cold start.
Raw artifacts preserve facts but reduce structured traversability.

artifacts.content:
ALTER TABLE release_packets ADD COLUMN approval_snapshot JSONB NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb;
-- Raw migration adds release_packets.approval_snapshot as immutable JSONB evidence.
-- Raw SQL evidence stays in the evidence layer and is not loaded on cold start.
-- Raw artifacts preserve facts but reduce structured traversability.

artifacts.title: Harbor system overview
artifacts.summary: Use the root layer for regulated change-control purpose, authority posture, and first-hop routing.

section.topic: regulated change-control purpose and authority posture

facts.text: Harbor Change Control coordinates regulated production changes for clinical data systems and external auditor review.
Human-primary SOP pages are allowed only when the pair declares sync_source=human-primary explicitly.
Cold-start routing begins at harbor-root and summary routing continues through harbor-capsule.

artifacts.title: Harbor capsule routing
artifacts.summary: Use the capsule for quick change-control orientation.

section.topic: harbor capsule routes to detailed authority

facts.text: Use harbor-change-control for approval rules, exception handling, and sign-off authority.
Use harbor-audit-evidence for evidence bundles, retention policy, and audit query references.

artifacts.title: Change policy brief
artifacts.summary: State mandatory fields, rollback discipline, and human-primary SOP posture for regulated change control.

facts.text: Every material change request must declare regulated asset, risk class, approver set, rollback owner, and evidence bundle.
Emergency changes require a documented rollback owner before execution.
The regulator-facing SOP is the declared human-primary surface for Harbor change control.

artifacts.title: Risk class matrix
artifacts.summary: Capture how regulated change classes alter approval depth.

artifact.usage: Regulated change approval depth by risk class.

facts.text: Risk class III changes require clinical QA and data stewardship approval.
Risk class II changes require data stewardship approval before scheduling.
Risk class I changes can auto-approve only when no schema, PHI, or retention behavior changes.

rows: Risk class I changes can auto-approve only when no schema, PHI, or retention behavior changes.
Risk class II changes require data stewardship approval before scheduling.
Risk class III changes require clinical QA and data stewardship approval.

artifacts.title: Approval decision tree
artifacts.summary: Map regulated change signals to approval paths.

facts.text: Emergency changes require retrospective approval within twenty-four hours.
Changes that alter PHI retention cannot skip security review.
Waivers expire at the next review board checkpoint.

nodes.action: Changes that alter PHI retention cannot skip security review.
Emergency changes require retrospective approval within twenty-four hours.
Waivers expire at the next review board checkpoint.

artifacts.title: Exception handling flow
artifacts.summary: Define how regulated exceptions are opened, approved, and closed.

facts.text: Exception flow records sponsor, approving authority, expiry time, and remediation task.
Expired exceptions block further deployment until closed.
Approved exceptions attach validation evidence before activation.

steps.action: Exception flow records sponsor, approving authority, expiry time, and remediation task.
Approved exceptions attach validation evidence before activation.
Exception remains active until its planned expiry.
Expired exceptions block further deployment until closed.

artifacts.title: Audit evidence overview
artifacts.summary: Describe how evidence bundles and blocked states behave under audit review.

facts.text: Every approved change links ticket evidence, validation evidence, and communication evidence.
Missing evidence keeps a change in blocked state even after technical rollout completes.

artifacts.title: Evidence retention window
artifacts.summary: Represent retention and audit snapshot timing.

facts.text: Audit evidence is retained for seven years.
Nightly evidence snapshots run at 02:15 UTC.
Legal hold suppresses deletion even after the normal retention window expires.

patterns.target: Audit evidence is retained for seven years.

patterns.on_trigger: Nightly evidence snapshots run at 02:15 UTC.

patterns.target: Legal hold suppresses deletion even after the normal retention window expires.

artifacts.title: Audit evidence query
artifacts.summary: Keep low-level evidence query text as an explicit raw escape hatch.

artifact.usage: Escape hatch for auditor-oriented SQL evidence.

facts.text: Auditors can query change approvals by change_id, approver_role, and evidence_status.
The evidence query is stored as raw SQL because benchmark escape hatches must stay measurable.
Evidence query snapshots are immutable once attached to an approved change record.

artifacts.content:
SELECT change_id, approver_role, evidence_status
FROM audit_change_approvals
WHERE approved_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days';
-- Auditors can query change approvals by change_id, approver_role, and evidence_status.
-- The evidence query is stored as raw SQL because benchmark escape hatches must stay measurable.
-- Evidence query snapshots are immutable once attached to an approved change record.

roles.scope: Needs goals, roadmap, verification, and summary routing.
Needs topology, APIs, workflow semantics, and impact analysis.
Needs policy, incidents, temporal controls, and evidence references.
Needs broad authoring context across every paired surface.
Needs regulated change policy, human-primary SOP alignment, exception handling, and audit evidence rules.
